source_jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer , Fullstack
source_jobs.description:
This role is based out of our Amsterdam office 🇳🇱 or London office 🇬🇧

We are a hybrid company & believe great products are made when we are together.



ABOUT STACKS

Stacks is building an AI-native platform that reinvents how modern finance teams run the monthly close. The opportunity is massive: every growing company depends on accurate, timely financial data, yet most are still operating with tools and workflows built for a different era.

In just two years, we’ve grown from a team of 2 to 30+ and are on a rapid trajectory towards $10m ARR. We’ve raised a $23m Series A led by Lightspeed Venture Partners https://stacks.ai/resources/stacks-raises-23-million-to-reinvent-finance-operations-with-agentic-ai, and we were recently recognised as one of Harmonic’s Hottest Startups of 2026 https://harmonic.ai/hot-25-companies/q2-2026.

We’re already trusted by leading European scale-ups including Pleo, Freetrade, and Motorway.

Our team is a blend of finance, product, and technical experts from top-tier companies like Uber, Plaid, Miro, Mollie, and Bunq, united by the drive to create a game-changing solution. We’re a team that is deeply customer-obsessed, plays to win, and isn’t afraid to take big, bold bets. We move fast, hold a high bar, and are committed to growing together as we scale.

🔎 About the Role

At Stacks, a Founding Product Engineer is a customer-obsessed, product-focused engineer who owns outcomes end to end, from customer conversation to production. You are driven by results, not tasks. You are passionate about solving real customer problems and constantly iterating to create elegant solutions.

🧱 What You'll Do

 - Operate as a high-agency engineer who owns outcomes, not just implementation — driving features from idea to production, making thoughtful product tradeoffs with PMs, leading rollout and adoption, jumping on customer pain points early, and pushing for a seamless product experience.

 - You will join customer calls alongside product, sit with finance leads, learn what is actually painful, and come back with a plan to fix it. The insights you gather directly shape what you build next.

 - You will make architectural decisions that matter, define data models, design APIs, and set patterns the rest of the team builds on. When something breaks in production, you own it.

 - As one of the earliest engineers on the team, you will also help set the bar. The way you write code, review others, and approach problems will define the culture of the team as it grows.

Our product and technology are always evolving, so being a fast learner matters more than knowing our stack on day one. Here is what we are currently building with:

Frontend: React, Next.js

Backend: Node.js, Express, TypeScript, Python, Protobuf

Data and Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, Google Cloud Platform, Terraform

🧩 What You Need

Ownership and DRI mindset: You own problems end to end, create clarity when none exists, and take responsibility for outcomes without waiting to be told what to do next.

Full stack depth with a backend focus: Strong TypeScript and Node.js fundamentals, comfortable across the stack, solid on API design, async processing, failure handling, and data modelling.

Judgment under ambiguity: You make high-quality trade-offs between speed, correctness, and long-term system health. You choose simple and scalable where others over-engineer.

Product and customer mindset: You think in customer problems, not tickets. You have talked to users and used what you learned to change what you built.

Learning speed: You close gaps independently and with urgency. You do not need a long ramp-up or structure to orient yourself.

Execution and reliability: You have shipped things that mattered and felt personally responsible when they did not work. You design for failure from the start.

Influence and leverage: You make the engineers around you faster and better through how you write code, share context, and raise issues.

🚀 What’s in It for

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager - Accounting
source_jobs.description:
This role is based out of our Amsterdam office 🇳🇱 or London office 🇬🇧


We are an office-first company & believe great products are made when we are together.



ABOUT STACKS

Stacks is building an AI-native platform that reinvents how modern finance teams run the monthly close. The opportunity is massive: every growing company depends on accurate, timely financial data, yet most are still operating with tools and workflows built for a different era.

In just two years, we’ve grown from a team of 2 to 30+ and are on a rapid trajectory. We’ve raised a $23m Series A led by Lightspeed Venture Partners https://stacks.ai/resources/stacks-raises-23-million-to-reinvent-finance-operations-with-agentic-ai, and we were recently recognised as one of Harmonic’s Hottest Startups of 2026 https://harmonic.ai/hot-25-companies/q2-2026.

We’re already trusted by leading European scale-ups, including Pleo, Freetrade, and Motorway.

Our team is a blend of finance, product, and technical experts from top-tier companies like Uber, Plaid, Miro, Mollie, and Bunq, united by the drive to create a game-changing solution.

We’re a team that is deeply customer-obsessed, plays to win, and isn’t afraid to take big, bold bets. We move fast, hold a high bar, and are committed to growing together as we scale.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Stacks is looking for a fiercely ambitious, entrepreneurial, and commercially driven Customer Success Manager - Accounting, to join our team as one of our first CS hires.



In this high-impact role, you’ll contribute to create and execute our GTM strategy at Stacks. If you’re excited by the idea of limitless growth and fast-paced challenges, this is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career as you help shape the future of Stacks.




🧱 WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Drive Customer Success: Support the full customer journey from onboarding to renewal and expansion. You’ll interact directly with Controllers, Finance leaders, and CFOs to ensure Stacks becomes mission-critical to their accounting operations.

 - Accounting-Led Support: Leverage your Accounting / Controller background to deeply understand customer workflows and challenges. You’ll offer guidance rooted in your financial close experience, helping customers maximize the value of Stacks.

 - Drive Net Retention: You will own renewals and expansion across your portfolio by delivering measurable value and identifying opportunities for deeper adoption.

 - Product Mastery: Become a power user of Stacks, able to guide customers through best practices, troubleshoot issues, and ensure seamless implementation.

 - Cross-functional Collaboration: Partner with Sales, Product, and Engineering to translate feedback into actionable improvements that address the needs of accounting teams.

 - Strategic Impact: As one of our first CS hires, you’ll help define the function from the ground up, building playbooks and influencing our GTM strategy.




🧩 WHAT YOU NEED

 - 3–7 Years of SaaS Customer Success Experience: A strong track record managing post-sales relationships with mid-market and enterprise clients. You’ve owned renewals, driven expansion, and worked cross-functionally to deliver success.

 - Accounting Expertise: Hands-on experience in accounting, ideally as a Controller, /ACA/ACCA, or within a finance team managing the close process. You speak the language of Stacks users and understand their day-to-day challenges.

 - Customer-Centric Mindset: You know how to build trust with finance teams, navigate and deliver strategic support.

 - Product Mastery: You’re quick to learn new tools and can confidently guide others through them. You can break down complex workflows into actionable steps for our customers..




🚀 WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU

 - Foundational role: Help shape a category-defining company from an early stage, working directly with founders and a small, high-agency team.

 - Meaningful ownership: Competitive equity in a company scaling rapidly towards $10m ARR, where your impact will

In this high-impact role, you’ll contribute to create and execute our GTM strategy at Stacks. If you’re excited by the idea of limitless growth and fast-paced challenges, this is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career as you help shape the future of Stacks.

 - Accounting Expertise Meets Customer Focus: Leverage your deep accounting knowledge to advise prospective and existing customers on best practices & help them translate complex accounting workflows into solutions using Stacks.

 - Sales Engineering Excellence: Partner with Sales, Customer Success & Engineering to lead technical discovery, deliver demos, and drive successful POCs that win deals.

 - Seamless Implementation: Lead implementation for new customers, ensuring they are fully onboarded and confident in using the product to transform their monthly close process.

 - Cross-functional Collaboration: Work directly with product and engineering to translate real-world accounting needs into product features and enhancements

 - Strategic Growth: As an early team member, you’ll help define Solutions Engineering at Stacks, building playbooks, processes, and eventually future teammates.





🧩 WHAT YOU NEED

 - 3–7 Years in Solutions Consulting: You've led technical sales cycles in a SaaS environment, and bring hands-on experience from an accounting or controller background.

 - Accounting Domain Knowledge: You’ve worked in or closely with accounting teams, ideally as a Controller, CPA, or someone deeply familiar with the month-end close process, reconciliations, and reporting.

 - Customer-Facing Confidence: Comfortable engaging with senior finance leaders and technical stakeholders alike. You know how to listen, challenge assumptions, and earn trust.

 - Product Mastery: Quick to understand and explain complex systems. You’re able to turn accounting pain points into clear product solutions.




🚀 WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU

 - Foundational role: Help shape a category-defining company from an early stage, working directly with founders and a small, high-agency team.

 - Meaningful ownership: Competitive equity in a company scaling rapidly towards $10m ARR, where your impact will directly shape the outcome.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative - Mid Market & Enterprise
source_jobs.description:
This role is based out of our Amsterdam office 🇳🇱 or London office 🇬🇧

We are an office-first company & believe great products are made when we are together.




ABOUT STACKS

Stacks is building an AI-native platform that reinvents how modern finance teams run the monthly close. The opportunity is massive: every growing company depends on accurate, timely financial data, yet most are still operating with tools and workflows built for a different era.



In just two years, we’ve grown from a team of 2 to 30+ and are on a rapid trajectory. We’ve raised a $23m Series A led by Lightspeed Venture Partners https://stacks.ai/resources/stacks-raises-23-million-to-reinvent-finance-operations-with-agentic-ai, and we were recently recognised as one of Harmonic’s Hottest Startups of 2026 https://harmonic.ai/hot-25-companies/q2-2026.



We’re already trusted by leading European scale-ups including Pleo, Freetrade, and Motorway. Our team is a blend of finance, product, and technical experts from top-tier companies like Uber, Plaid, Miro, Mollie, and Bunq, united by the drive to create a game-changing solution.



We’re a team that is deeply customer-obsessed, plays to win, and isn’t afraid to take big, bold bets. We move fast, hold a high bar, and are committed to growing together as we scale.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Stacks is looking for a fiercely ambitious, entrepreneurial, and commercially driven Business Development Representative to join as one of our initial GTM hires!



In this high-impact role, you’ll collaborate closely with our Sales team to create and execute our GTM strategy at Stacks. If you’re excited by the idea of limitless growth and fast-paced challenges, this is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career as you help shape the future of Stacks.





🧱 WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Prospecting & Pipeline Generation: Own outbound into Mid-Market and Enterprise companies across the UK.

 - Outbound Strategy: Run multi-channel campaigns across email, phone, and LinkedIn. You'll be writing, testing, and iterating constantly.

 - Discovery & Qualification: Hold sharp, insight-led conversations with finance leaders. Your consulting instincts for asking the right questions will show up here.

 - Product Mastery: Get deep on the close process fast, so you can speak credibly about debits, credits, reconciliations, and the pain of a 10-day close.

 - Cross-functional Partnership: Work directly with our Sales team to shape messaging and build a repeatable outbound motion.

 - Growth Path: Clear track to AE. This is the launchpad role. You're looking at an 8-12 month trajectory into a mid-market deal-making role.




🧩 WHAT YOU NEED

 - Brilliant Background: 1–3 years in a top-tier environment like MBB, VC, PE or Early-stage

 - Analytical Horsepower: You think in frameworks, get to the "so what" quickly, and can break down a prospect's business in a first call.

 - Commercial Instinct: You want to be closer to revenue. You're motivated by tangible, measurable outcomes, not slide decks read by three people.

 - Hunger & Grit: You're competitive, coachable, and don't flinch at a cold call.

 - Clear Communicator: Written, spoken, and on the phone, you can be concise, direct, and persuasive.




🚀 WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU

 - Foundational role: Help shape a category-defining company from an early stage, working directly with founders and a small, high-agency team.

 - Meaningful ownership: Competitive equity in a company scaling rapidly towards $10m ARR, where your impact will directly shape the outcome.

 - Cutting-edge product and tech: Work at the intersection of AI and finance, building systems that fundamentally change how companies run their financial close.

 - High-calibre team: Collaborate with people from companies like Uber, Plaid, Miro, and Mollie.

 - Exceptional workspace: Our London HQ is based in Fora — a modern, design-led space in the heart of the city with access to a gym, classes, and an environment built for focused, in-person bu

source_jobs.title: Senior Talent Partner - GTM
source_jobs.description:
THIS ROLE IS BASED OUT OF OUR LONDON OFFICE 🇬🇧



We are an office-first company and believe great products are built when teams work closely together.




ABOUT STACKS

Stacks is building an AI-native platform that reinvents how modern finance teams run the monthly close. The opportunity is massive: every growing company depends on accurate, timely financial data, yet most are still operating with tools and workflows built for a different era.



In just two years, we’ve grown from a team of 2 to 30+ and are on a rapid trajectory. We’ve raised a $23m Series A led by Lightspeed Venture Partners https://stacks.ai/resources/stacks-raises-23-million-to-reinvent-finance-operations-with-agentic-ai, and we were recently recognised as one of Harmonic’s Hottest Startups of 2026 https://harmonic.ai/hot-25-companies/q2-2026.



We’re already trusted by leading European scale-ups including Pleo, Freetrade, and Motorway. Our team is a blend of finance, product, and technical experts from top-tier companies like Uber, Plaid, Miro, Mollie, and Bunq, united by the drive to create a game-changing solution.



We’re a team that is deeply customer-obsessed, plays to win, and isn’t afraid to take big, bold bets. We move fast, hold a high bar, and are committed to growing together as we scale.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Stacks is hiring a Senior Talent Partner – GTM to support our next phase of growth.

While your immediate focus will be scaling our Go-To-Market organisation, we're looking for someone who can flex across the business as hiring priorities evolve. You'll bring strong experience hiring commercial talent, while being equally comfortable partnering on operational, product or technical hiring when needed.

This is a hands-on role for someone who thrives in startup environments, enjoys wearing multiple hats, and loves building exceptional teams through proactive sourcing rather than relying on inbound applications.

Reporting to the Talent Lead, you'll partner closely with hiring managers and leadership to deliver outstanding hiring outcomes while helping shape how we attract, assess and hire exceptional talent as we grow.




🧱 WHAT YOU'LL DO


FULL-CYCLE RECRUITMENT

 - Own end-to-end hiring across a range of functions, with an initial focus on Sales, Marketing, Customer Success and Implementation.

 - Flex across the business as hiring priorities evolve, supporting roles outside of GTM where needed.

 - Manage multiple live searches simultaneously while maintaining a high bar for quality.

 - Build high-quality talent pipelines through outbound sourcing, referrals, networking and creative talent mapping.

 - Screen, assess and guide candidates through the interview process through to offer.

 - Deliver an exceptional candidate experience from first touch through to close.


HIRING MANAGER PARTNERSHIP

 - Partner closely with hiring managers to define hiring strategies and calibrate on talent.

 - Drive interview processes, feedback loops and hiring decisions with pace and clarity.

 - Provide market insights on talent, competition and hiring trends.

 - Act as a trusted advisor throughout the hiring process, balancing speed with quality.


TALENT & MARKET INTELLIGENCE

 - Identify and engage exceptional commercial talent across the UK and Europe.

 - Build talent pipelines for both immediate hiring needs and future growth.

 - Share insights on compensation, talent availability and competitor hiring activity.

 - Continuously improve sourcing strategies and hiring practices as we scale.




🧩 WHAT YOU NEED

 - Significant experience recruiting within high-growth startups or scale-ups (this is essential).

 - Strong experience hiring across Go-To-Market functions including Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, Partnerships or Revenue Operations.

 - The versatility to recruit beyond GTM as business priorities evolve—you see yourself as a great recruiter first, with a strength in commercial hiring.

 - Exceptional sourcing skills with a track r

 - Finance SaaS Selling Experience: You’ve sold into the Office of the CFO, ideally, financial reporting, planning, ERP, or close automation tools, and understand how to speak their language.

 - Flawless Sales Execution: You know how to prospect, qualify, run discovery, tailor demos, negotiate, and close. Calm operator in longer sales cycles with political complexity

 - Strong Product Acumen: You're quick to understand complex tools and can explain them in a clear, compelling way that resonates with both finance and technical stakeholders.

 - Entrepreneurial Energy: You’re excited to build from the ground up. You thrive in ambiguity, move fast, and bring structure where needed. Evidence building territories from scratch

We’re a team that is deeply customer-obsessed, plays to win, and isn’t afraid to take big, bold bets. We move fast, hold a high bar, and are committed to growing together as we scale.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Stacks is hiring a Product Lead to own and build out one of our most strategically important product areas from the ground up.



This isn't a traditional PM role. We're looking for someone who thinks and operates like a founder — someone who will take full ownership of a product area, drive it to product-market fit, and create the commercial momentum to get it to its first million in revenue.



You'll be the connective tissue across GTM, engineering, design, customer success, and external stakeholders. You'll go deep on customer problems, set the strategic direction, and move with pace and urgency to make it happen.



We're looking for a hyper-ambitious builder with sharp product instincts, first-principles thinking, and the commercial chops to put themselves in front of senior enterprise stakeholders and win.




🧱 WHAT YOU'LL DO

Own Your Product Area End-to-End

 - Take full ownership of a core product area (e.g. Accounts Receivable) — from strategy through to delivery and commercial outcomes

 - Define product vision and roadmap grounded in deep customer insight and first-principles thinking

 - Drive urgency and business impact, with a clear line of sight to product-market fit and revenue

 - Set the direction, prioritise ruthlessly, and hold the bar high across everything you touch

Be the Founder of Your Product

 - Think and operate like a co-founder: take initiative, unblock yourself, and own outcomes regardless of what's in your job spec

 - Build selling processes and GTM motion around your product area from scratch

 - Put yourself in front of senior enterprise stakeholders — run the meetings, bring the narrative, and close the loop

 - Work through ambiguity and complexity, breaking hard problems into clear, actionable pieces

Connect the Company Around Your Product

 - Act as the hub connecting engineering, design, GTM, CS, and external domain experts

 - Partner closely with engineering to define what to build and why — without needing to be managed

 - Bring structure, focus, and pace to a cross-functional team working on something genuinely new

 - Create alignment and energy across the organisation around your product area

Drive Customer Obsession

 - Get deep on customer problems: uncover insights, challenge assumptions, and keep the customer at the centre of every decision

 - Build relationships with customers and domain experts who can sharpen your thinking

 - Translate complex workflow challenges into elegant, scalable product solutions




🧩 WHAT YOU NEED

 - A genuine builder mindset — high ownership, relentless follow-through, and a track record of creating things from scratch

 - Strong product fundamentals: you know

source_jobs.title: Implementation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
This role is based out of our Amsterdam office 🇳🇱 or London office 🇬🇧


We are an office-first company & believe great products are made when we are together.



ABOUT STACKS

Stacks is building an AI-native platform that reinvents how modern finance teams run the monthly close. The opportunity is massive: every growing company depends on accurate, timely financial data, yet most are still operating with tools and workflows built for a different era.

In just two years, we’ve grown from a team of 2 to 30+ and are on a rapid trajectory. We’ve raised a $23m Series A led by Lightspeed Venture Partners https://stacks.ai/resources/stacks-raises-23-million-to-reinvent-finance-operations-with-agentic-ai, and we were recently recognised as one of Harmonic’s Hottest Startups of 2026 https://harmonic.ai/hot-25-companies/q2-2026.

We’re already trusted by leading European scale-ups including Pleo, Freetrade, and Motorway.

Our team is a blend of finance, product, and technical experts from top-tier companies like Uber, Plaid, Miro, Mollie, and Bunq, united by the drive to create a game-changing solution.

We’re a team that is deeply customer-obsessed, plays to win, and isn’t afraid to take big, bold bets. We move fast, hold a high bar, and are committed to growing together as we scale.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Stacks is looking for a fiercely ambitious, entrepreneurial, and technically driven Implementation Engineer to join our team!


In this high-impact role, you’ll be critical in ensuring our customers successfully implement Stacks, integrating it with their core financial systems. If you’re excited by the idea of limitless growth and fast-paced challenges, this is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career as you help shape the future of Stacks.




🧱 WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Product Implementation & Onboarding: Lead the technical implementation of Stacks for new customers and new product features/modules, including configuring the environment, executing system tests, assisting with UAT, and providing cutover and hypercare support to ensure they are fully onboarded and proficient in using Stacks.

 - Customer Solutions & Support: Leverage technical knowledge to advise customers on best practices, translate accounting business problems into solutions using Stacks, and work with the Engineering team to diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve complex technical issues during and after deployment.

 - Process Improvement & Automation: Drive function building and process improvements to establish smoother, more efficient operational workflows between Customer Success and Engineering. Develop internal tools and automation scripts (light scripting/coding) to streamline customer implementations and scale operational efficiency.

 - Documentation & Self-Service: Develop and refine Stacks' documentation hub by updating existing content, creating new documentation, and adding materials that support improved client self-service.

 - Product Improvement: Drive continuous product improvement by capturing and structuring customer feedback, collaborating directly with Product and Engineering to articulate clear problem statements, and influencing the prioritisation of future development.




🧩 WHAT YOU NEED

 - Project Management: Experience managing software implementation projects (3+ years) in a SaaS environment, either as an internal lead for process improvement initiatives or as an external consultant managing client implementations.

 - ERP Expertise: Deep, hands-on experience using Accounting ERPs as a power user (Accountant or ERP Administrator) or as an external Consultant who has supported client implementations and improvements. Experience with NetSuite and Microsoft Dynamics 365 is a plus.

 - Technical Proficiency: Can understand and explain complex systems to customers, and can self-solve technical problems encountered by customers. Experience with SQL is a plus, as is a basic understanding of software development principles and the ability to cre

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Stacks

Stacks is building an AI-native platform that reinvents how modern finance teams run the monthly close. The opportunity is massive: every growing company depends on accurate, timely financial data, yet most are still operating with tools and workflows built for a different era.

In just two years, we’ve grown from a team of 2 to 30+ and are on a rapid trajectory towards $10m ARR. We’ve raised a $23m Series A led by Lightspeed Venture Partners https://stacks.ai/resources/stacks-raises-23-million-to-reinvent-finance-operations-with-agentic-ai, and we were recently recognised as one of Harmonic’s Hottest Startups of 2026 https://harmonic.ai/hot-25-companies/q2-2026.

We’re already trusted by leading European scale-ups including Pleo, Freetrade, and Motorway.

Our team is a blend of finance, product, and technical experts from top-tier companies like Uber, Plaid, Miro, Meta, Mollie, and Bunq, united by the drive to create a game-changing solution.

We’re a team that is deeply customer-obsessed, plays to win, and isn’t afraid to take big, bold bets. We move fast, hold a high bar, and are committed to growing together as we scale.

🔎 About the Role

Stacks is looking for a Staff Product Engineer to set the technical standard for how we build. This is not a management role. It is an individual contributor role with outsized influence, where your decisions shape architecture, unblock teams, and define what great looks like across the entire engineering organization. You will work at the intersection of complex technical problems and real customer outcomes. You will be the person others come to when the problem is hard, when the trade-off is unclear, or when the stakes are high. You will define the approach, not just execute it

🧱 What You'll Do

 - Own the most technically complex and highest-impact problems in the product, end to end, from architectural vision through to production reliability.

 - Define technical direction across the engineering team: set patterns, establish standards, and make foundational decisions that others build on for years.

 - Leads cross-functional technical discussions, partnering closely with product, design, and the founding team to translate ambiguous problems into elegant, scalable solutions.

 - Join customer calls and engage directly with finance leads and CFOs. You will bring technical depth to commercial conversations and use what you learn to sharpen what we build.

 - Identify and resolve systemic risks before they become production incidents. You design for failure, not around it.

 - Raise the bar for the whole team: through code reviews, technical mentorship, and by modelling what rigorous, thoughtful engineering looks like in practice.

 - Lead by example as an active contributor: not only setting technical direction, but consistently writing production code, raising the bar in code reviews, mentoring engineers, and evolving AI-native engineering workflows.

Our product and technology are always evolving, so being a fast learner matters more than knowing our stack on day one. Here is what we are currently building with:

Frontend: React, Next.js

Backend: Node.js, Express, TypeScript, Python, Protobuf

Data and Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, Google Cloud Platform, Terraform

🧩 What You Need

Ownership and DRI mindset: You own problems end to end, create clarity when none exists, and take responsibility for outcomes without waiting to be told what to do next.

Full stack depth with a backend focus: Strong TypeScript and Node.js fundamentals, comfortable across the stack, solid on API design, async processing, failure handling, and data modelling.

Judgment under ambiguity: You make high-quality trade-offs between speed, correctness, and long-term system health. You choose simple and scalable where others over-engineer.

Product and customer mindset: You think in customer problems, not tickets. You have talked to users and used what you learned to change what you built.

Lea

notes: Rapidly growing AI-native platform for finance teams, with a strong focus on product development and engineering roles.
